brief_document.title: run_server bootstrap integration test with outbox.enabled
brief_document.purpose: The durable-outbox fan-out path already exists (OutboxStore trait + libsql impl, append_with_outbox, the OutboxDispatcher task in aion-server, and crash-recovery rearm_outbox_pending). What is missing is an end-to-end integration test that boots run_server with outbox.enabled=true and proves a fan-out workflow dispatches its members THROUGH the outbox table and the OutboxDispatcher and completes exactly once — including across a mid-flight restart. This locks in the outbox contract before the remaining outbox hardening (#9-11) and the later liminal swap (#13) build on it. See docs/AION-OUTBOX-BUILD-PLAN.md Phase 4.
brief_document.task: Add a black-box integration test that starts a real run_server (or the closest existing test harness) configured with outbox.enabled=true and outbox.batch_size set, runs a fan-out workflow (collect_all over N children), and asserts the members are dispatched via the outbox (rows transition pending -> claimed -> done) and all complete exactly once. Add a second scenario that restarts the server mid-flight (between dispatch and completion) and asserts recovery re-arms the pending outbox rows and the workflow still completes with no duplicate terminal events and no double-wake. Reuse existing server/test harnesses and the existing ConnectedWorkerRegistry path; do not re-implement the outbox.

requirements.title: Happy-path outbox fan-out integration test
requirements.spec: WHEN run_server is started with outbox.enabled=true and a valid outbox.batch_size, and a fan-out workflow (collect over N members) is executed, THE SYSTEM SHALL dispatch every member through the outbox table and the OutboxDispatcher, and all N members SHALL complete exactly once. THE TEST SHALL observe the outbox row lifecycle (pending -> claimed -> done) via the store, not via internal mocks.
acceptance: A new integration test (tests/ in aion-server or the appropriate crate) boots run_server with outbox.enabled=true + an explicit outbox.batch_size.
A fan-out workflow over N>=3 members runs to completion through the outbox path.
The test asserts outbox rows reach status=done for all members and the workflow's terminal/collected result is correct.
No duplicate terminal events are recorded in history for any member.

requirements.title: Restart-mid-flight recovery scenario
requirements.spec: WHEN the server restarts after outbox rows are appended but before all members complete, THE SYSTEM SHALL re-arm the pending outbox rows on recovery (rearm_outbox_pending) and the fan-out SHALL still complete exactly once, with no duplicate terminal events and no double-wake of the workflow.
acceptance: A test scenario restarts (or simulates restart of) the server between member dispatch and completion.
After restart, pending outbox rows are re-armed and the remaining members complete.
The collected result is correct and the history contains exactly one terminal per member (duplicate completion is Dropped, not double-recorded).

boundaries: TEST-ONLY: SHALL NOT change outbox logic, the OutboxStore trait, the dispatcher, or the Recorder. If a genuine bug is found, REPORT it clearly in the result; do not silently work around it.
SHALL reuse existing run_server / test harnesses and the existing worker registry; do not re-implement dispatch.
SHALL NOT introduce flaky timing: use deterministic waits / store polling, not fixed sleeps where avoidable.
SHALL keep no production unwrap()/expect()/panic! outside tests; respect the aion workspace lint policy (deny).
SHALL NOT add new public API surface.
verification: cargo fmt --check clean for changed files.
cargo test passes in full including the new integration test(s); zero failures, no ignored-without-reason.
The happy-path test genuinely exercises outbox.enabled (fails if the outbox path is disabled/broken).
The restart scenario genuinely exercises recovery re-arm (fails if rearm is removed).

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines; extract submodules if approaching the limit.
No panics in production code; unwrap()/expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains only pub mod / pub use; no logic.
All public types implement Debug; all error types implement std::error::Error.

resolved_context.intention: Lock in the durable-outbox fan-out contract with an end-to-end test before further outbox hardening (#9-11) and the liminal dispatch swap (#13). The outbox machinery already exists; this proves it under run_server + restart. See ABLATIVE-STACK-ROADMAP.md and docs/AION-OUTBOX-BUILD-PLAN.md.

provenance.quote: keep on going on those things so long as everything is properly documented
